Mr. Leonard Panish 
Registrar-Recorder 
5557 Ferguson Drive 
Post Office Box 30450 
Los Angeles, California 90030 
Dear Mr. Panish: 
July 29, 1981 
This is in regard to Assembly Bill 516 (Robinson) which 
was recently signed into law by the Governor and is effective 
on January 1, 1982. 
This new law provides that candidates who have filed a 
declaration stating that less than $500 in contributions will 
be received or made in behalf of or in support of his or her 
own candidacy, will not be required to file semi-annual 
campaign statements. 
Therefore, candidates in a June or November elections, 
would also be allowed to file a Form 470, Candidates and 
Officeholders Short form, if they did not anticipate receiving 
or expending less than $500 in behalf of their candidacies 
during the calendar year. 
It is the view of the staff of 
the Commission that, (1) since the bill has already been 
signed into law, and, (2) the language of the form 470 currently 
states these same requirements, we will allow candidates filing 
in connection with the November 3, 1981 election to use the form 
470 if they do not anticipate receiving or expending $500 or 
more in behalf of their· own candidacies. 
If we can be of any further assistance, please do not 
hesitate to contact our office.

sections.conclusion: It is the view of the staff of the Commission that, (1) since the bill has already been signed into law, and, (2) the language of the form 470 currently states these same requirements, we will allow candidates filing in connection with the November 3, 1981 election to use the form 470 if they do not anticipate receiving or expending $500 or more in behalf of their own candidacies.
sections.question_synthetic: May candidates in the November 3, 1981 election use Form 470 (short form) if they do not anticipate receiving or expending $500 or more in campaign contributions and expenditures, given the recent enactment of Assembly Bill 516?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC staff determines that candidates in the November 3, 1981 election may use Form 470 (short form) if they do not anticipate receiving or expending $500 or more, based on Assembly Bill 516 and the existing form language. This allowance is granted because the bill is already signed into law and Form 470 already contains these same requirements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a correspondence/advice letter from FPPC staff to an official regarding implementation of AB 516. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present, but clear Q/A structure exists. Form and requirements are referenced but not fully quoted. Letter is addressed to Registrar-Recorder and cc'd to George Vertelney.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May candidates in the November 3, 1981 election use Form 470 (short form) if they do not anticipate receiving or expending $500 or more in campaign contributions and expenditures, given the recent enactment of Assembly Bill 516?

CONCLUSION: It is the view of the staff of the Commission that, (1) since the bill has already been signed into law, and, (2) the language of the form 470 currently states these same requirements, we will allow candidates filing in connection with the November 3, 1981 election to use the form 470 if they do not anticipate receiving or expending $500 or more in behalf of their own candidacies.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises the Los Angeles County Registrar-Recorder that Assembly Bill 516, effective January 1, 1982, allows candidates to use the short form (Form 470) if they do not anticipate $500 or more in campaign activity, and permits candidates in the November 1981 election to use this form immediately.
